title: Cosmic Turbine
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Device |image= |name=Cosmic Turbine |manufacturer= |model= |type= |culture= |creators=[[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |created=By [[30,000 BBY]] |modifier= |destroyed=[[4250 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |discovered=4250 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] |owners= |locations=[[Vultar system/Legends|Vultar system]]{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 39: The Third Great Schism}} |cost= |value= |structure= |size=Immense{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 01: The Architects}} |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight=Massive |protection= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose=[[Gravity/Legends|Gravity]] manipulation |heritage= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|But Old Republic records chronicle the Vultar Cataclysm, an event in which the activation of an artifact known as the Cosmic Turbine destroyed that system. I suspect this Turbine was of Celestial origin.|Edited transcript of the debriefing of Doctor Insmot Bowen|{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}}} The '''Cosmic Turbine''' was an immense device capable of manipulating [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] on a large scale. Part of a set of massive machines located in the [[Vultar system/Legends|Vultar system]], it was created by the ancient [[Celestial/Legends|Celestial]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. When a group of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] took up residence in the Vultar [[Star system/Legends|system]] in [[4250 BBY]], they attempted to use the Cosmic Turbine and other devices against their enemy, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. However, a catastrophic accident after the technology's activation [[Vultar Cataclysm|destroyed]] the entire Vultar system, including the Dark Jedi and the Celestial machinery. Tens of [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] [[1 BBY/Legends|later]], the Cosmic Turbine was discussed by the [[Obroan Institute for Archaeology]] researcher [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Insmot Bowen]] when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] debriefed them on the Celestials. ==Description== {{Quote|But I think I understand—in broad outline, mind you—how the Celestials did what they did. I think their Turbines were tractor-repulsors built to move planets and stars through hyperspace.|Edited transcript of the debriefing of Doctor Insmot Bowen|}} The Cosmic Turbine was an extraordinary, immense machine that was a part of a set of massive devices located in the [[Vultar system/Legends|Vultar system]]. The powerful{{CSWECite|I|148|Cosmic Turbine}} Cosmic Turbine and another creation of the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestial]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system's]] [[Centerpoint Station]], could manipulate [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] on a large scale via the use of [[hyperspace tractor beam]]s, [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsor]] pulses, and [[Wormhole/Legends|hyperspace wormholes]]. ==History== {{Quote|Doctor, are you saying you believe the Celestials built the Corellian system using this… Galactic Engine?''"
"''Cosmic Turbine. It's possible, General. Though I wonder if the Turbine might have been part of a network of such devices, most of them now dismantled or destroyed—or perhaps yet to be found, out there in the Unknown Regions.|General Arhul Kurumenga and Doctor Insmot Bowen, edited transcript of Bowen's debriefing|}} The Cosmic Turbine was created by the Celestials at some point before the latter disappeared from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in [[30,000 BBY]]. Evidence pointed to the Celestials having used the Cosmic Turbine and its fellow devices to construct the Vultar [[Star system/Legends|system]], including its [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. [[File:Vultar-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Dark Jedi activation of the Cosmic Turbine led to the destruction of the Vultar system.]] In [[4250 BBY]], after a group of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] were driven from the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the [[Third Great Schism]], they took up residence in the Vultar system. The [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] adherents discovered the Celestial machinery there, in the process giving the Cosmic Turbine its name. Seeking revenge against their [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] foes and intending to unleash as much destruction as possible,{{CSWECite|I|xii|A Timeline of Galactic Events}} the Dark Jedi activated the Cosmic Turbine and the rest of the devices, but control over the machines proved to be far beyond their abilities. Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] into the [[darksider]]s' attempt to use the technology, a catastrophic accident that came to be known as the [[Vultar Cataclysm]] [[Mundicide|destroyed]] the entire Vultar system, including the Dark Jedi and the machines themselves. The event came to be chronicled in [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] records, and by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the Republic's successor, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Obroan Institute for Archaeology]] [[Pre-Republic era]] specialist [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Insmot Bowen]] suspected that the Cosmic Turbine had been of Celestial origin. In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Imperial authorities debriefed Bowen on the Celestials. When [[General/Legends|General]] [[Arhul Kurumenga]] inquired during the briefing as to whether the Celestials had used the Cosmic Turbine—which the general erroneously referred to as the "Galactic Engine"—to construct the Corellian system, the researcher pointed it out as a possibility, further speculating that the artifact could have been a part of a network of similar, [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]–equipped devices that Bowen referred to as "Turbines" and that most of the rest of those machines either had been dismantled, destroyed, or remained undiscovered in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. The Cosmic Turbine was also mentioned in a [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|document]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]], published in [[36 ABY]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Cosmic Turbine was first mentioned in the [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. It was originally meant to be mentioned in the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]]; however, the section that would have mentioned the artifact was cut before the book's release. The information regarding the Cosmic Turbine was later released in [[2013]] as part of [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]], a series of articles published on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars Blog|Blog]] by Fry and [[Del Rey]] editor [[Erich Schoeneweiss]] containing various cut content from ''The Essential Guide to Warfare''. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}} {{Codex}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2005/10/endnotes-for-star-wars-new-essential.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The New Essential Chronology}} {{Interlang |de=Kosmikturbine |es=Turbina Cósmica |deGA=1 }} [[Category:Gravitational technology]] [[Category:Objects created by the Celestials]] [[Category:Vultar system locations]]
